blocks.content: A moment of silence followed.
Though it wasn't their last chance, they knew that if this didn't work, they would ultimately be the ones to lose.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Would it work?
It was a gamble, almost a 50-50 chance.
The biggest issue was that they were currently in a space created by the Regulation Exception.
How much of a gap was there between the Orga in front of them and the real fairy Orga?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If the impact was even slightly indirect, the operation would fail.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is an incredibly complex and delicate Regulation Exception.
The entire building was materialized, with numerous Regulation Exceptions installed in each room.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Was it even possible?
No human could distort so many objects to this extent.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it were possible…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The flow of consciousness.
Shirone recalled his experience with Drimo.
Only the space they were currently standing in was materialized, while the other rooms existed in a state of nothingness.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was like thinking about your house in your mind, not viewing it all at once but letting your thoughts flow as if you were moving through it.
If that was the case, it was possible to contain another Regulation Exception within a Regulation Exception.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Though it's not an easy task…
Therefore, Orga's consciousness was focused on this room.
This was the hypothesis Shirone had formed.
Having organized his thoughts, Shirone proposed to Orga.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't take your eyes off me for one minute.
It was a strange proposal.

blocks.speaker: Orga
blocks.content: Alright. Let's do that.
Shirone stared intently at Orga.
Once this act was over, it would be Orga's turn.
The outcome would be decided there.
Valhalla Action.
In a situation bound by the Regulation Exception, actions outside the rules were likely prohibited.
But Valhalla Action might be possible.
The equivalent exchange of cause and effect would accept even the cause of the prohibition and convert it into a result.
Therefore, the key was the calculation time.
Even with Ataraxia's accumulation time reduced to less than 30 seconds, Shirone proposed one minute to buy some extra time.
As Shirone performed the proposal, Orga stared at him.
Staring meant Orga's mind was fully focused on Shirone.
Clang!
The magic circle of Valhalla Action appeared above his head. The calculation was instantaneous.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's working!
Exactly 57 seconds.
That was the time it took for Ataraxia to accumulate while under the effect of Mirroring.
Before Orga could say anything in surprise, the cause and effect reversed, and a dazzling Ataraxia appeared before them.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Kyah!
Even the head fairy of Shehakim couldn't withstand the magic circle of the archangel, especially Ikael, the highest authority in Heaven.
In fact, the stronger the fairy, the greater the impact.
Cracks appeared on Orga's body, and light began to seep out.
The seal of light grew in size, eventually engulfing her and oxidizing intensely.
Rumble!
As Paradise collapsed, the original scenery was revealed.
It was still Paradise, but the plaster on the walls had all shattered and fallen to the floor.
The fragments of plaster were inscribed with various complex patterns painted in red.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: She designed the space and controlled it with her mind.
Plu felt relieved.
If a fairy could materialize a building to this extent with a Regulation Exception, humanity would have no chance in this war.

blocks.speaker: Orga
blocks.content: Ugh!
Orga lay on the ground, her small, frail body writhing in pain.

blocks.speaker: Fairies
blocks.content: Director! Are you alright?
Numerous fairies assisting Orga rushed to her side, shouting in concern.
Then they turned to Shirone's group with terrifying eyes.

blocks.speaker: Fairies
blocks.content: They're rebel spies! Kill them!
As the fairies flew at them, Plu stood in front of Shirone.
She didn't know how long the price of Valhalla Action would last, but she had to fight until Shirone could move.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Phoenix Formation!
Plu raised the firebird high and opened the Dawkins Algorithm to face the fairies.
The fairies of Shehakim were among the top ranks of the 72 classes, and fighting them felt like battling a group of professional magicians from the human world.
Flames spewed out, and the different concepts of the fairies shook Paradise.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: I can't hold out for long!
The limitation of the Dawkins Algorithm lay in the magician's physical abilities.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: No matter how automatic the reactions were, if subjected to continuous attacks like this, her body wouldn't hold up.
54 seconds.
55 seconds.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Each time, Plu's body twisted grotesquely, and her joints screamed in pain.

blocks.speaker: Fairies
blocks.content: Kill them! If we capture them, the war will end!
The rebels' firepower was formidable even to Heaven, but capturing Shirone, the Light of Sector 73, would crush the enemy's morale.
Orga's strategy, deducing the espionage tactics from the battle on Shehakim's outskirts, was worthy of being called a fairy strategist.
56 seconds.
When the fairy of loud noise fired a sonic wave, it sounded like hundreds of tons of steel beams were being pushed aside right next to her, disrupting Plu's concentration.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Ugh!
As the Dawkins Algorithm broke, dozens of fairies rushed in from all sides.
Exactly 57 seconds!
At the same time, Shirone sprang forward from the ground.
Just as the fairy of brute strength was about to crush Plu's head, a blur passed by, and instead, her body exploded.
Then the surrounding fairies began to shatter into pieces.
Orga, barely regaining her senses, stared blankly at Shirone with eyes hidden beneath heavy makeup.
Ugh!

blocks.speaker: Orga
blocks.content: Director! You shouldn't move yet!

blocks.speaker: Orga
blocks.content: Request… request support.
Meanwhile, Shirone continued to eliminate all the visible fairies.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Over there!
Before the fairies could request support, the giant unit charged in, with the immortal Kergos among them.
They had once been part of the hunting squad, making their combat power overwhelming even among their kind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone! We should retreat for now!
Given that the premise of stealth had been broken, it was best to follow Plu's advice, but escaping now was impossible.
He had already experienced the Kergos' tracking skills in Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmph!
Shirone glared at the enemy.
An artificial brain eye emerged, and Akamai's Antithesis bound them.
Ugh!
The immense restraining force made even the giants and fairies tremble in shock.
Though a fallen angel, the restraining force that could immobilize the angelic Ikael was not something mere giants could break.
With the added magic amplification trait of the true demon species Galatomic, a photon cannon several times larger than before appeared before Shirone.
The photon cannons multiplied like shadow clones, and finally, twelve massive spheres of light shook, emitting a brilliant glow.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now!
Shirone fired all the photon cannons and turned his body.
Boom, boom, boom, boom, boom!
A deafening explosion erupted in Sector 12 of Shehakim, and light flashed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hurry!
Shirone and Plu leaped over the Paradise buildings and fled.

blocks.speaker: Fairies
blocks.content: Chase them! Don't let them escape!
The surviving Kergos quickly pursued them.
The chase lasted for 30 minutes.
Skima's physical abilities shone in urban warfare, and Shirone and Plu managed to evade their pursuers by setting a complex route.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huff, huff!
The two leaned against a wall, gasping for breath.
Arman increased their oxygen saturation, quickly stabilizing their breathing, and Shirone's broken finger had already healed.
It was thanks to Kenser's trait of infinite cell regeneration.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What do we do now?

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: We go to Araboth.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: But we don't know where we are. We're lost. And…
The enemies already knew Shirone had infiltrated.
The difficulty of the mission had skyrocketed compared to the beginning.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: First, let's find some clothes.
Though they had been severely affected by Orga's Regulation Exception, they couldn't walk around in their current state.

blocks.speaker: Feope
blocks.content: I found you, Shirone.
At the voice from the sky, Shirone and Plu's faces turned pale.
As the voice reached them, a small creature entered their Spirit Zone.
A small creature. A fairy.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: We have to kill it!
Plu turned, and Shirone swung his tendrils faster.
The fairy flinched at the sudden attack.
Shirone was able to stop the tendrils because Arman had enhanced his dynamic vision to the extreme.
The sharp needle of the tendril stopped right in front of the fairy's eyes.
Seeing the fairy trembling with her eyes tightly shut, Shirone removed his hood with a blank expression.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You, you…?
The spiral fairy, Feope, who had helped Shirone until the end during his last visit to Heaven, slowly opened her eyes.
Eek!
Feope recoiled in fear at the needle in front of her.

blocks.speaker: Feope
blocks.content: What are you doing! Are you trying to kill me?
Shirone quickly retracted the tendrils.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Feope! It's you…
More than anything else, Shirone felt a surge of relief and joy. In the midst of the battlefield, seeing Feope's face brought tears to his eyes.
As Shirone moved closer, as if to embrace her, Feope hastily extended her hands, feigning indifference.

blocks.speaker: Feope
blocks.content: W-What are you doing? Why are you acting like this all of a sudden?

blocks.speaker: Feope
blocks.content: How did you find me? How did you know I was here? Is it okay for you to be here?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The rational part of Shirone's mind considered the possibility that Feope might be an enemy. But he pushed that thought aside for now.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If that assumption were true, he wouldn't be able to bear the consequences, no matter the sacrifice.

blocks.speaker: Feope
blocks.content: I'm glad you're here, Shirone. I've missed you.
Feope cupped Shirone's cheeks and kissed his forehead.
As the warmth of her lips spread, Shirone's mind was filled with the memory of her voice from before.

blocks.speaker: Feope
blocks.content: I love you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if it was the confession of an immature fairy whose concepts hadn't fully developed, her sincerity was unmistakable.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Feope. It's really you.
Shirone gently lifted Feope into his hands.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Feope.

blocks.speaker: Feope
blocks.content: Yes?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I have to fight against Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Feope
blocks.content: Alright. I'll take you there.
